title: Moldy Filled Italian Rice Suppli
description: A tangy, juicy, quick-fix Italian dish from Piedmont, featuring blueberries and namul-blanch-season, made in a copper pot, and served as a snack.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a copper pot over medium heat.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the onion and cook for 3 minutes, until softened.
Add the cooked white rice and stir to coat with oil and mix with onion and garlic.
Add the namul-blanch-season and mix well.
Add the dairy-free mozzarella shreds and mix until they start to melt.
Add the sliced moldy fungi and mix to combine.
Transfer the mixture to a bowl and let it cool to room temperature.
Once cooled, stir in the chopped parsley, salt, and black pepper.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Just before serving, stir in the blueberries and vinegar.
Spoon the mixture into pre-cooked rice balls (supplì) and serve immediately.
